channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Here are the District of Columbia's 2026 primary election results
item.description: Live election results: Get the latest on U.S. delegate and mayoral primary races in Washington, D.C.
item.content:encoded: Live election results: Get the latest on U.S. delegate and mayoral primary races in Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Former ICE leader lands new job consulting on national security and defense
item.description: Because of his former job, Todd Lyons cannot engage with the Department of Homeland Security for a year, per federal law.
item.content:encoded:
Because of his former job, Todd Lyons cannot engage with the Department of Homeland Security for a year, per federal law.

(Image credit: Samuel Corum)
item.dc:creator: Sergio Martínez-Beltrán

item.title: Soccer Edition: Watch World Cup highlights from across the NPR Network
item.description: The 2026 World Cup is playing out in communities across the country. Journalists from NPR and its member stations are in your city — capturing the excitement and asking the important questions.
item.content:encoded: The 2026 World Cup is playing out in communities across the country. Journalists from NPR and its member stations are in your city — capturing the excitement and asking the important questions.
item.dc:creator: The NPR Network

item.title: As the U.S. turns 250, this historian has blunt advice: 'America has to grow up'
item.description: In America, U.S.A., Princeton historian Eddie Glaude Jr. looks at the country through the lens of its previous anniversaries and centennials. "The divided soul of the nation is in full view," he says.
item.content:encoded:
In America, U.S.A., Princeton historian Eddie Glaude Jr. looks at the country through the lens of its previous anniversaries and centennials. "The divided soul of the nation is in full view," he says.

(Image credit: Lisa Lake)
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: Former American businessman detained in Myanmar after alleged financial misconduct
item.description: Adam Castillo, founder of AGS Myanmar, was detained Thursday at Yangon International Airport. The U.S. State Department is aware of a reported detention but hasn't commented further.
item.content:encoded:
Adam Castillo, founder of AGS Myanmar, was detained Thursday at Yangon International Airport. The U.S. State Department is aware of a reported detention but hasn't commented further.

(Image credit: Gemunu Amarasinghe)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: As Luigi Mangione's lawyers head to court, support grows for the accused 'vigilante'
item.description: Luigi Mangione's legal team is back in court in New York City this week for a key pretrial hearing. He's accused of stalking and killing an insurance CEO. Donors have given $1.5 million to support his defense.
item.content:encoded:
Luigi Mangione's legal team is back in court in New York City this week for a key pretrial hearing. He's accused of stalking and killing an insurance CEO. Donors have given $1.5 million to support his defense.

(Image credit: Leon Neal)
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann

item.title: Trump announces deal to end war in Iran. And, how to stay safe in deadly heat
item.description: Trump says a deal has been reached to end the war between the U.S. and Iran. Plus, this is what extreme temperatures do to the human body — and how you can keep yourself safe.
item.content:encoded:
Trump says a deal has been reached to end the war between the U.S. and Iran. Plus, this is what extreme temperatures do to the human body — and how you can keep yourself safe.

(Image credit: Chris Graythen)
item.dc:creator: Suzanne Nuyen

item.title: Britain will ban under-16s from social media apps, including TikTok and YouTube
item.description: The ban will apply to platforms including Snapchat, TikTok, YouTube, Instagram, Facebook and X. The move makes the U.K. part of a growing global movement to tighten online safety for children.
item.content:encoded:
The ban will apply to platforms including Snapchat, TikTok, YouTube, Instagram, Facebook and X. The move makes the U.K. part of a growing global movement to tighten online safety for children.

(Image credit: Carlos Jasso)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. and Iran announce an initial deal to end the war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The deal is a major breakthrough in the conflict that set the Middle East aflame and shook the global economy. However, it did not resolve critical issues set aside for further negotiations.
item.content:encoded:
The deal is a major breakthrough in the conflict that set the Middle East aflame and shook the global economy. However, it did not resolve critical issues set aside for further negotiations.

(Image credit: Mandel Ngan)
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: Most parents track their 18- to 25-year-old kids on their smartphones. Is it healthy?
item.description: A new survey from the University of Michigan asks parents about their use of technology to track their adult children, ages 18-25, including using "always on" location tracking on their smartphones.
item.content:encoded: A new survey from the University of Michigan asks parents about their use of technology to track their adult children, ages 18-25, including using "always on" location tracking on their smartphones.
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey
